name: ASocks
tagline: Budget residential SOCKS5 pool with flexible targeting
strengths: Low entry cost for residential SOCKS5 and HTTP access
Flexible geo-targeting down to country, region, and city
Simple dashboard with per-port and rotating session options
Good fit for smaller-scale scraping and account tasks
weaknesses: Smaller, less battle-tested network than tier-1 providers
Support and documentation are lighter than enterprise vendors

name: Bright Data
tagline: Enterprise-grade proxy and web data platform
strengths: One of the largest residential IP pools in the industry
Full stack: residential, ISP, datacenter, and mobile proxies
Advanced tools like Web Unlocker, SERP API, and scraping browsers
Granular geo-targeting, strong compliance, and KYC controls
Highly reliable network with enterprise SLAs and uptime
weaknesses: Premium pricing that is steep for solo users and small teams
Dashboard and product breadth have a steep learning curve

name: DataImpulse
strengths: Very low per-GB pricing with no forced monthly commitment
Ethically sourced residential pool with global coverage
Traffic never expires, which suits sporadic usage
Straightforward setup for scraping and multi-accounting
weaknesses: Newer brand with a smaller pool than market leaders
Fewer advanced unlocking and API products

name: Evomi
strengths: Aggressive per-GB pricing across residential, mobile, and datacenter
Swiss data-privacy positioning and ethically sourced IPs
Free trial and low minimums lower the barrier to test
Clean dashboard with modern rotating and sticky sessions
weaknesses: Smaller network and shorter track record than incumbents
Enterprise tooling and support depth are still maturing
poolSize: tens of millions of residential IPs

name: Infatica
tagline: Balanced mid-market residential and mobile proxies
strengths: Competitive pricing with residential, mobile, and datacenter tiers
Solid global coverage and country-level targeting
Bundled scraper API and data-collection tooling
Ethical sourcing program and reasonable support
weaknesses: Success rates can trail tier-1 pools on the hardest targets
Pool size is smaller than the largest enterprise vendors

name: IPRoyal
tagline: Flexible proxies with non-expiring residential traffic
strengths: Residential traffic that does not expire on pay-as-you-go plans
Broad lineup: residential, mobile, ISP, datacenter, and sneaker
Competitive entry pricing and low minimum top-ups
Responsive support and a beginner-friendly dashboard
weaknesses: Pool depth on niche geos is thinner than tier-1 providers
Advanced unlocking features are limited versus enterprise suites

name: LunaProxy
tagline: Low-cost residential pool with strong geo coverage
strengths: Aggressive per-GB residential pricing for the pool size
Wide country and city-level targeting options
Unlimited-traffic and rotating plans for high-volume users
Simple onboarding suited to scraping and social tasks
weaknesses: Newer vendor with a shorter reliability track record
Support depth and tooling lag behind market leaders

name: NetNut
tagline: ISP-backed proxies built for speed and stability
strengths: Direct ISP-sourced residential and static ISP proxies
Fast, stable connections with high session persistence
Strong for large-scale, latency-sensitive data collection
Enterprise support with dedicated account management
weaknesses: Pricing is oriented toward business and enterprise budgets
Fewer self-serve, low-commitment entry options

name: Nimbleway
tagline: AI-driven web data collection and unblocking
strengths: Smart unblocking with AI-assisted request handling
Structured web-data APIs beyond raw proxy access
Reliable residential network with good success rates
Developer-friendly platform for automated pipelines
weaknesses: Premium, platform-oriented pricing over raw bandwidth
Overkill for simple, low-volume proxy needs
poolSize: tens of millions of residential IPs

name: NodeMaven
tagline: High-quality sticky sessions and clean IPs
strengths: IP-quality filtering that favors clean, low-flag addresses
Long sticky sessions ideal for account management
Residential and mobile pools with granular targeting
Non-expiring traffic and beginner-friendly setup
weaknesses: Smaller pool than tier-1 enterprise providers
Limited advanced scraping and unlocking tooling

name: Oxylabs
tagline: Premium enterprise proxies and scraper APIs
strengths: Massive, high-quality residential and datacenter pools
Powerful Web Scraper API and Web Unblocker products
Excellent success rates on difficult, protected targets
Enterprise SLAs, dedicated support, and strong compliance
Granular geo-targeting with city and ASN precision
weaknesses: Premium pricing aimed at businesses over hobbyists
Full platform can feel complex for first-time users

name: PacketStream
strengths: One of the lowest per-GB residential prices on the market
Simple pay-as-you-go model with no monthly minimums
Easy setup for basic scraping and lightweight automation
Traffic credits that suit intermittent, low-volume use
weaknesses: Smaller pool and lower success rates on hard targets
Limited geo-targeting granularity and few advanced features
Speed and stability trail premium networks

name: Proxy-Seller
tagline: Versatile proxies with strong dedicated IP options
strengths: Wide range: residential, mobile, ISP, and dedicated datacenter
Strong choice for individual dedicated IPv4 and IPv6 proxies
Country and city targeting with flexible rental periods
Good value and responsive customer support
weaknesses: Residential pool is smaller than tier-1 vendors
Interface and product naming can feel dated
poolSize: 15M+ residential IPs plus large dedicated inventory

name: ProxyEmpire
tagline: Rollover traffic and deep mobile proxy coverage
strengths: Residential and 4G/5G mobile proxies with broad geo reach
Rollover traffic so unused bandwidth is not lost
Granular targeting by country, region, city, and ISP
Ethically sourced pool with flexible rotation controls
weaknesses: Per-GB pricing runs higher than budget competitors
Pool depth trails the largest enterprise networks

name: Rayobyte
tagline: Datacenter proxy specialist with ethical sourcing
strengths: Deep, affordable dedicated and rotating datacenter pools
Ethically sourced residential option and scraping tools
Generous replacement and IP-swap policies
US-based support with strong reputation for reliability
weaknesses: Residential pricing is less competitive than its datacenter tier
Residential pool is smaller than tier-1 providers
poolSize: Large datacenter fleet plus millions of residential IPs

name: Smartproxy
strengths: Large residential pool with high success rates
Polished dashboard and easy onboarding for beginners
Competitive per-GB pricing with generous entry plans
Broad product range including scraping APIs and site unblocker
Strong documentation and responsive support
weaknesses: Enterprise controls are lighter than the largest vendors
Peak pricing can rise on the smallest bandwidth tiers

name: SOAX
tagline: Clean residential and mobile pool with fine targeting
strengths: Whitelisted, regularly cleaned residential and mobile IPs
Very granular targeting by country, region, city, and carrier
Flexible rotation and sticky-session controls
Good balance of quality, coverage, and support
weaknesses: Per-GB pricing sits in the premium mid-market range
Smallest plans are less cost-efficient than bulk tiers

name: Webshare
tagline: Cheap datacenter proxies with a free starter tier
strengths: Extremely affordable datacenter and residential proxies
Free plan and self-serve dashboard for instant testing
Fast, developer-friendly setup with API access
Flexible bandwidth and proxy-count scaling
weaknesses: Shared datacenter IPs get blocked on tough targets
Support is largely self-serve with limited hands-on help
Residential pool is smaller than premium vendors
startingPrice: Free tier available; datacenter from ~$1/proxy; residential from ~$7/GB
poolSize: 30M+ residential IPs plus large datacenter fleet
